paper.title: Blue extreme disk-runaway stars with Gaia EDR3

extraction.summary: Identified 7 stars that the paper treats as gravitationally unbound or possibly unbound from the Milky Way: S5-HVS 1, HVS 5, HVS 6, B481, LAMOST HVS 1, HVS 7, LAMOST HVS 4.

method_chain.summary: Gaia EDR3 astrometry (proper motions) combined with spectrophotometric distances and radial velocities derived from quantitative spectral analysis (this work and previous publications).

source_refs.context: Based on proper motions from Gaia EDR3, we then carried out spectroscopic and kinematic analyses

method_chain.summary: Spectrophotometric distances derived from fitting model spectra and spectral energy distributions to observed photometry; typical uncertainties <10%.

source_refs.context: we used spectrophotometric distance estimates as input for our kinematic investigation

method_chain.summary: Radial velocities measured from optical spectra (various spectrographs) via spectral fitting.

source_refs.context: Atmospheric parameters... radial velocity were determined by fitting ... observed spectra

method_chain.summary: Solar position and motion relative to the local standard of rest adopted from Schönrich+2010; exact values (R0, z0, v_circ, U, V, W) not reported in this paper.

parameters.name: R0
parameters.description: Distance of Sun to Galactic centre not explicitly stated; Monte Carlo simulations incorporate uncertainties from Schönrich+2010.

source_refs.context: distance of the Sun to the GC and in its relative motion to the local standard of rest (Schönrich+2010)

method_chain.summary: Computed galactocentric positions and velocities from spectrophotometric distances, radial velocities, and Gaia EDR3 proper motions.

source_refs.context: combined with proper motions from Gaia EDR3 to trace back the trajectories ... to derive their ejection velocities

method_chain.summary: Adopted Milky Way mass model I of Irrgang+2013 (three-component axisymmetric potential).

parameters.name: potential_name
parameters.raw_value: Milky Way mass model I of Irrgang et al. (2013)
parameters.description: Galactic potential model used for orbit integration and escape velocity definition.

source_refs.context: we used the Milky Way mass model~I of \citetads{2013A&A...549A.137I}

method_chain.summary: Backward numerical integration of orbits to the Galactic plane using a fourth-order Runge-Kutta-Fehlberg method; disk height distribution modelled with varying scale height depending on galactocentric radius.

source_refs.context: equations of motions are numerically solved ... backward integration ...

method_chain.summary: Computed Galactic rest-frame velocities, local escape velocities, ejection velocities, and bound probability P_b from Monte Carlo simulations (10^6 runs).

source_refs.context: ejection velocities ... and ... P_b are listed

method_chain.summary: Quantitative spectrophotometric analysis: fitted model spectra to observed spectra to derive T_eff, log(g), v sin i, then computed synthetic SED to derive angular diameter, extinction, stellar mass, radius, luminosity, and age.

source_refs.context: Atmospheric parameters ... were determined by fitting ... Stellar masses M and ages were inferred by comparing ... to evolutionary tracks ... spectrophotometric distance d finally followed ...

method_chain.summary: Classified spatial origin (GC, disk, outer disk) based on galactocentric plane-crossing radius r_p.

source_refs.context: Three subgroups ... possible origin in the GC ... origin in the Galactic disk ... possible origin in the outer rim ...

inclusion_assessment.summary: S5-HVS 1 is the most extreme object, gravitationally unbound from the Milky Way, probable Hills mechanism ejection from the Galactic center.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper explicitly states 'the only object that is gravitationally unbound to the Milky Way' in the GC origin subsection and gives P_b=0%.

source_refs.context: It is also the only object that is gravitationally unbound to the Milky Way.
overwhelming observational evidence ... found by \citetads{2020MNRAS.491.2465K}. They discovered that the A-type star S5-HVS\,1 travels ...
S5-HVS\,1 can be considered as evidence for the Hills mechanism

inclusion_assessment.summary: HVS 5 is a gravitationally unbound disk runaway ('hyper-runaway') with a well-constrained origin near the solar circle.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper explicitly states 'clearly gravitationally unbound to the Milky Way' and gives P_b=0%.

source_refs.context: HVS\,5 is certainly one of the most interesting targets because it is, on the one hand, clearly gravitationally unbound to the Milky Way
MMT-HVS survey \citepads{2014ApJ...787...89B}
its place of origin is now so precisely constrained to lie in the current solar neighborhood

inclusion_assessment.summary: HVS 6 is gravitationally unbound from the Milky Way, ejected from the outer disk.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper states 'HVS 6 is unbound to the Milky Way' and gives P_b=0%.

source_refs.context: HVS\,6 is unbound to the Milky Way
MMT-HVS survey \citepads{2014ApJ...787...89B}
HVS\,6 is unbound to the Milky Way while B481 and HVS\,7 might be.

inclusion_assessment.summary: B481 is an outer-rim disk runaway that the paper considers possibly gravitationally unbound to the Milky Way.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper says B481 'might be' unbound; P_b=60%.

source_refs.context: while B481 and HVS\,7 might be
MMT-HVS survey \citepads{2014ApJ...787...89B}
B481 ... might be

inclusion_assessment.summary: LAMOST HVS 1 is a hyper-runaway star (gravitationally unbound from the Milky Way) originating from the disk.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper describes LAMOST HVS 1 as a hyper-runaway star (unbound disk runaway) and P_b=2%.

source_refs.context: LAMOST HVS\,1 and LAMOST HVS\,4 are two further examples for such so-called hyper-runaway stars
the four candidate HVSs from the LAMOST survey (\citeads{2014ApJ...785L..23Z}; \citeads{2017ApJ...847L...9H}; \citeads{2018AJ....156...87L})
LAMOST HVS\,1 and LAMOST HVS\,4 are two further examples for such so-called hyper-runaway stars

inclusion_assessment.summary: HVS 7 is an outer-rim runaway that the paper considers possibly gravitationally unbound to the Milky Way.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper says HVS 7 'might be' unbound; P_b=42%.

source_refs.context: HVS\,6 is unbound to the Milky Way while B481 and HVS\,7 might be
MMT-HVS survey \citepads{2014ApJ...787...89B}
HVS\,7 might be

inclusion_assessment.summary: LAMOST HVS 4 is a hyper-runaway star (gravitationally unbound from the Milky Way) originating from the disk.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper describes LAMOST HVS 4 as a hyper-runaway star (unbound disk runaway) and P_b=0%.

source_refs.context: LAMOST HVS\,1 and LAMOST HVS\,4 are two further examples for such so-called hyper-runaway stars
the four candidate HVSs from the LAMOST survey (\citeads{2014ApJ...785L..23Z}; \citeads{2017ApJ...847L...9H}; \citeads{2018AJ....156...87L})
LAMOST HVS\,4 are two further examples for such so-called hyper-runaway stars

candidate_groups_considered.description: All 30 program stars listed in Table~1 with ejection velocities, galactocentric radii at plane intersection, and stellar masses.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: Only objects explicitly described as gravitationally unbound or possibly unbound (S5-HVS 1, HVS 5, HVS 6, B481, LAMOST HVS 1, HVS 7, LAMOST HVS 4) are included; the remaining 23 stars are not considered possibly unbound by the paper.

source_refs.context: Table caption and contents of ejection velocity, galactocentric radius ...
